_comment_1: Everything this system knows about minimart. No Java file may contain any of it.
_comment_2: Flat JSON with dotted keys, read one exact-quoted-needle at a time by the repo's own scanner. A nested document would need a JSON library, which would be the first dependency in a repo whose argument is that it has none.
_comment_identity: 900000, NOT 1000. minimart's own seeded population uses customer ids from 1000 up, and its subscribe endpoint is idempotent per (tenant, customer, variant): it hands back whatever subscription that customer already holds. A visitor reusing those ids would silently land on somebody else's subscription, inherit its renewal date, and every measurement afterwards would be of a subscription the visitor never made. Identity spaces must be disjoint by construction.
_comment_clock: Business time is posted by us, once per tick, and never by a visitor. minimart accepts an absent business_at and defaults to now, so a run that forgot to send it would be pinned to the wall clock and quietly irreproducible.
_comment_precheck: Whoever posts /api/sim/tick owns the clock. If minimart's own population is running it will advance business time underneath this run, and the result is a run that looks fine and is quietly invalid.
precheck.1.note: minimart's own simulation must not be running: two owners of one clock is not a race, it is nonsense
BROWSE.description: list what is for sale. The only honest way to learn a product id.
CHECK_SUBS.description: check what you are actually subscribed to, rather than assuming
_comment_subscribe_refusal: minimart CANNOT express refusal here. MartApi.subscribe has one catch that answers 400 for a malformed business_at and 500 for everything else, so a bad product id and a database outage are the same response. That is a real limitation of the platform, recorded rather than papered over: a 500 is classified ERROR, which is honest, and it means REFUSED is unreachable on this endpoint.
SUBSCRIBE.description: subscribe to a product you have seen
CANCEL.description: cancel a subscription you hold. Only ids you have actually seen.
